comment: Published measurement inputs for the TFPT fixed-point watchdog. All values are public, published numbers; retrieval dates 2026-07-02 (axes A-D) and 2026-07-03 (axes E-G). TFPT constants are NOT in this file -- they come from the axioms/ledger and live in the CLI.

neutrino_mass.source: DESI DR2 BAO + CMB neutrino constraints (Elbers et al., arXiv:2503.14744, 2025)
neutrino_mass.lcdm_upper_95_note: Sum m_nu < 0.0642 eV (95%), LCDM, DESI DR2 BAO + CMB (CamSpec); plik gives 0.0691, L-H 0.0774
neutrino_mass.lcdm_feldman_cousins_note: boundary-corrected 95% Feldman-Cousins upper bound; breaches the normal-ordering oscillation floor
neutrino_mass.lcdm_eff_note: effective cosmological neutrino mass parameter (allows negative energy density, removes prior-weight effects); paper quotes 3.0 sigma tension with the NO oscillation floor 0.059 eV
neutrino_mass.w0wacdm_note: in w0waCDM the bound relaxes to 0.163 eV (95%) and the effective-mass posterior peaks at 0 -- the neutrino tension is resolved by evolving dark energy
neutrino_mass.oscillation_note: normal-ordering lower limit from oscillations as used by DESI (approx. 0.059 eV)

dark_energy_cpl.source: DESI DR2 Results II (arXiv:2503.14738, 2025), CPL w0-wa best fits, DESI BAO + CMB + one SN compilation at a time; same values as experiments/dark-energy-w-watchdog
dark_energy_cpl.overlap_note: the three SN compilations share low-z SNe and are ALTERNATIVE datasets -- never stack them
dark_energy_cpl.h0_note: H0 used only to convert d ln rho_DE / d ln a to a per-year rate (order-of-magnitude scale setting; DESI DR2 LCDM baseline H0 = 68.36 +- 0.29, Planck 67.4; the axis-B conclusion is insensitive to this choice at the quoted precision)

alpha_drift_clock.source: PTB Yb+ E3/E2 optical clock comparison, Filzinger et al., Phys. Rev. Lett. 130, 253001 (2023), arXiv:2301.03433; data May 2016 - Nov 2022; still the strictest published limit as of 2026-07-02

lepton_masses.m_tau_source: PDG 2024 + 2025 update world average (S. Navas et al., Phys. Rev. D 110, 030001 (2024); rpp2025-sum-leptons): 1776.93 +- 0.09 MeV. NOTE: supersedes the older 1776.86 +- 0.12 (PDG 2022/2023).
lepton_masses.m_tau_belle2_source: Belle II 2023 (arXiv:2305.19116, PRD 108, 032006): 1777.09 +- 0.08(stat) +- 0.11(syst) MeV -- most precise single measurement, included in the PDG average

neutron_lifetime.source: beam-bottle status as of 2026-07-03: UCNtau final (arXiv:2409.05560, PRC 2025); beam average incl. NIST BL1 re-eval (Symmetry 16 (2024) 956 review); J-PARC electron-counting beam (arXiv:2412.19519); master formula |Vud|^2 tau_n (1+3 gA^2) = 4906.4(1.7) s (Czarnecki-Marciano-Sirlin update, arXiv:1907.06737 eq. 49); gA = 1.27641(56) (PERKEO III, arXiv:1812.04666); post-2002 average gA = 1.2762(5)
neutron_lifetime.tau_bottle_magnetic_avg_note: average of the three magnetic/gravitational UCN storage experiments (Symmetry 16 (2024) 956)
neutron_lifetime.tau_beam_avg_note: average of the three proton-counting beam results, dominated by NIST BL1 887.7 +- 1.2 +- 1.9 s
neutron_lifetime.tau_beam_jparc_note: J-PARC ELECTRON-counting beam (arXiv:2412.19519): 877.2 +- 1.7(stat) +4.0/-3.6(syst) -- a beam-method result already on the bottle side
neutron_lifetime.discrepancy_note: beam(p-counting) - bottle(UCN storage) = 9.8 +- 2.1 s (>4 sigma); exotic dark-decay explanations (n -> chi + ...) need a new light dark state

cabibbo_first_row.source: PDG 2026 rev. 'Vud, Vus, the Cabibbo Angle, and CKM Unitarity' (frozen 2026-07-03, same table as experiments/tfpt-discovery/electron_sector_cabibbo_probe.py)
cabibbo_first_row.note: the ~2.4 sigma first-row deficit is the third dissolution axis named 2026-07-03; tau-route V_us is a known FESR outlier, PDG26 flags it as methodologically contested

x17.source: X17/ATOMKI status as of 2026-07-03: ATOMKI 8Be/4He/12C anomalies (PRL 116 042501; PRC 104 044003; PRC 106 L061601); MEG II null (Eur. Phys. J. C 85 (2025) 763, arXiv:2411.07994); PADME Run III resonant scan (JHEP 11 (2025) 007); Barducci et al. JHEP 04 (2025) 035

atomki.reference: ATOMKI combined: MX = 16.85(4) MeV claimed in 8Be*, 4He*, 12C* internal-pair angular correlations
atomki.status_note: the originating anomalies persist at the source lab; interpreted as a protophobic ~17 MeV boson; no independent confirmation of the nuclear-transition excess

meg2.reference: MEG II, EPJ C 85 (2025) 763: 7Li(p,e+e-)8Be at 1080 keV, four weeks 2023
meg2.status_note: no significant signal; 90% CL limits R_17.6 < 1.8e-6, R_18.1 < 1.2e-5 (16.5-17.1 MeV) -- still compatible at ~1.5 sigma with the ATOMKI combination (Barducci+ 2025), so not yet a kill

padme.reference: PADME Run III, JHEP 11 (2025) 007: resonant e+e- scan sqrt(s) = 16.4-17.4 MeV
padme.status_note: blind analysis consistent with background over most of the range; largest deviation at 16.90 MeV with GLOBAL significance ~1.8-2.0 sigma (local 2.5). Run IV (2025/26, upgraded tracker + finer scan) underway -- the decisive dataset

rd_rdstar.source: HFLAV CKM 2025 average (hflav-eos, incl. LHCb PRL 134 061801 (2025) and Belle II PRD 112 032010 (2025)); SM: HFLAV arithmetic averages
rd_rdstar.note: R(D) +2.5 sigma, R(D*) +2.3 sigma individually; 3.8 sigma combined (3.5 with FLAG24 lattice SM). Belle II full dataset + LHCb Run 3 decide

sterile_neutrino.source: short-baseline anomaly status as of 2026-07-02: MicroBooNE two-beam (Nature 648, 64-69 (2025)); gallium/BEST reviews; JSNS2 first results (arXiv:2602.06274); Fermilab SBN program (ICARUS first results, Apr 2026)

microboone_two_beam.status_note: excludes the single light sterile (3+1) interpretation of the LSND and MiniBooNE anomalies at 95% CL; rules out a significant portion of the gallium/BEST parameter space. First use of two accelerator beams (BNB 0.57% intrinsic nu_e, NuMI 4.6%) to break the nu_e appearance/disappearance degeneracy.

gallium_anomaly.reference: SAGE/GALLEX calibrations + BEST 2022 (R_in = 0.79 +- 0.05, R_out = 0.77 +- 0.05); combined significance >~ 4-5 sigma
gallium_anomaly.status_note: the ~20% counting deficit itself PERSISTS unexplained; BEST sees no distance dependence (no oscillation signature), and the large mixing a sterile explanation needs is disfavoured by solar/reactor data; BEST-2 (58Co source) proposed.

jsns2.status_note: direct LSND test at J-PARC MLF: 2 events observed vs 2.3 +- 0.4 expected background (LSND anomaly would add 1.1 +- 0.5) -- no excess, not yet conclusive (~7% of approved POT analysed; ~half of planned POT accumulated by end 2025). JSNS2-II near+far detectors taking data since Nov 2025 (far detector commissioning).

sbn_program.reference: Fermilab SBN: ICARUS first nu_mu-disappearance results (2022-23 data, Apr 2026); SBND running
sbn_program.status_note: ICARUS sees no evidence of nu_mu disappearance and sets 90% CL limits on the 3+1 model; the joint SBND+ICARUS two-detector analysis is designed to decide the short-baseline anomalies this decade.
